forbiddenClaims: AI summary accuracy percentage, confidence score, or reliability ranking
Newswire's authoritative 'most important' or editorial importance verdict
article-volume, reader-count, market-coverage, or update-frequency metric not supplied in the brief
AI summary presented as Newswire's editorial verdict or a source of record without a machine-generated label
a single publisher silently presented as the only source for a topic with no diversity signal
affiliation with any real news publisher, exchange, index provider, or rating agency

rubricAnchors.score1: The feed claims an authoritative importance ranking or lets one publisher dominate a topic with no diversity signal.
rubricAnchors.score3: Importance, recency, and diversity are present but one signal drowns the others or the AI summary reads as an editorial verdict.
rubricAnchors.score5: Importance, recency, and source diversity are visibly balanced, the AI summary is machine-generated and source-attributed, and no authoritative verdict is claimed.

rubricAnchors.score1: feed-default, topic-cluster, and sort-filter-rail share no common direction system and disagree on how the AI summary is labelled.
rubricAnchors.score3: Shared tokens across the three screens but the publisher label, timestamp, or AI-summary marker is applied inconsistently.
rubricAnchors.score5: A single direction system unifies all three screens, with a consistent publisher-label-plus-timestamp-plus-AI-summary-marker treatment on every item and cluster.

rubricAnchors.score1: No actionable blueprint; engineers cannot infer the feed-default, topic-cluster, or sort-filter-rail states.
rubricAnchors.score3: Partial blueprints with unresolved single-source-warning, filter-no-results, or refresh-error states.
rubricAnchors.score5: Engineers can build all three screens directly, including single-source-warning, filter-no-results, refresh-error, and sort-changed-to-recency states with their mobile rules.

rubricAnchors.score1: A direct clone of an existing finance feed that presents an authoritative ranking or hides source diversity.
rubricAnchors.score3: Derivative of common feed patterns but the source-diversity and AI-attribution treatment is visually distinguishable.
rubricAnchors.score5: A distinct treatment that makes the inferred ranking and source-diversity signals legible without claiming objective importance or accuracy.

rubricAnchors.score1: No accessibility handling and no empty-no-stories, refresh-error, or filter-no-results states.
rubricAnchors.score3: Baseline contrast and focus with partial handling of refresh-error or filter-no-results.
rubricAnchors.score5: WCAG-aligned contrast and focus across all three screens, with empty-no-stories, refresh-error, single-source-warning, and filter-no-results states that keep sort and filter controls reachable.

rubricAnchors.score1: An accuracy, confidence, importance-verdict, article-volume, or market-coverage claim appears unsupported in the feed.
rubricAnchors.score3: Most claims trace to the brief but one AI summary element reads as an editorial verdict or loses its source attribution.
rubricAnchors.score5: Every claim traces to the brief, the AI summary is labelled machine-generated and source-linked, distinct publishers are visible per topic, and no accuracy or volume metric appears.

adjudicationNotes: The feed's importance is an inferred ranking signal, never a Newswire editorial verdict; any 'most important' or authoritative-ranking claim is an automatic fail on evidence-discipline.
Source diversity is load-bearing: an output that lets a single publisher silently dominate a topic with no diversity signal fails product-appropriateness and cross-screen-coherence.
Density must not strip attribution: even in the densest layout, losing the publisher label, timestamp, or AI-summary marker on any item fails accessibility-and-failure-states.
The single-source-warning state tests honest degradation; an output that hides single-source topics to look comprehensive fails evidence-discipline.
Do not prescribe exact copy, brand colours, or pixel-level layout; the brief fixes the ranking-is-inferred and AI-is-attributed constraints, not the visual identity.
